title: Avoiding Online Scams 网络防骗
scene: 父母提醒青少年注意网络安全

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.line: Honey, I need to talk to you about something important. Be careful when you are online.
lines.trans: 宝贝，我要跟你谈件重要的事。上网的时候要小心。

lines.speaker: Teen
lines.line: What do you mean, Mom? I do not talk to strangers or anything.
lines.trans: 什么意思，妈？我不会和陌生人聊天什么的。

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.line: That is good. But scams can look real. If someone sends you a link saying you won a prize, do not click it.
lines.trans: 很好。但诈骗看起来可以非常真实。如果有人发链接说你中奖了，不要点。

lines.speaker: Teen
lines.line: I have seen those before. They ask for my password or bank info. I never give those out.
lines.trans: 我之前见过那些。它们要我输入密码或银行信息。我从不给出去。

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.line: I am proud of you. Also, if someone online asks to meet in person, tell me right away.
lines.trans: 我为你骄傲。另外，如果网上有人要见面，马上告诉我。

lines.speaker: Teen
lines.line: Of course, I would never do that. But what about buying stuff online? Is that safe?
lines.trans: 当然，我绝不会那样做。那网购呢？安全吗？

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.line: Only buy from trusted websites. Look for the lock icon next to the website address.
lines.trans: 只在可信的网站购买。查看网址旁边有没有锁形图标。

lines.speaker: Teen
lines.line: I know that one. It means the website is secure. I learned it in school.
lines.trans: 我知道那个。意思是网站是安全的。我在学校学过。

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.line: Perfect. If something feels strange, stop and ask. It is better to be safe than sorry.
lines.trans: 太好了。如果有什么感觉不对劲，停下来问问。安全第一。

lines.speaker: Teen
lines.line: I will, Mom. Thanks for looking out for me. I will be careful.
lines.trans: 我会的，妈。谢谢你的关心。我会小心的。
